content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer array of size NNN.

Your task is to construct its **prefix array** (also known as the **running sum array**).

The prefix array PPP is defined such that each element represents the sum of all elements from the beginning of the array up to that position:

P[i]=A1+A2+⋯+Aifor 1≤i≤NP[i] = A_1 + A_2 + \dots + A_i \quad \text{for } 1 \le i \le NP[i]=A1​+A2​+⋯+Ai​for 1≤i≤NIn other words, the ithi^{\text{th}}ith element of the prefix array stores the sum of the first iii elements of the original array.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer NNN, the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers.


Output Format

Print NNN space-separated integers representing the prefix array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‑separated integers representing the prefix (running sum) array of the given array.

solution.approach: For each test case we iterate through the array once, maintaining a variable `cur` that stores the sum of elements seen so far. After reading each element we add it to `cur` and output `cur`. This yields the prefix sum in O(N) time per test case. No additional data structures are required beyond a few scalar variables.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) – each element is processed exactly once.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        cur = 0
        line = []
        for _ in range(n):
            cur += int(next(it))
            line.append(str(cur))
        out_lines.append(' '.join(line))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
